what_it_does: Registers the external CosyVoice repository and its CosyVoice2-0.5B model weights as a terrain entry, documenting how the Company voice engine imports it via sys.path and how the systemd unit launches it on port 4126.
observations: External git clone at /home/tim/CosyVoice, 5.3G, upstream Apache-2.0 remote https://github.com/FunAudioLLM/CosyVoice.git
Provides both upstream library code and pretrained_models/CosyVoice2-0.5B snapshot
Not directly executed; pulled in at runtime by adding the repo path to sys.path
company-voice-cosyvoice.service launches the in-repo engine voice/engines/cosyvoice.py using the cosyvoice venv Python on port 4126
Engine header documents the clone/sys.path/model-dir contract
Coverage shows files_read 2 / files_total 2 despite a 5.3G repository
Status is unconfirmed even though last read and service activity dates are present
No secrets flagged and move_intent is none
standouts: Runtime sys.path injection rather than installed package dependency
Multi-gigabyte pretrained model weights tracked as part of an external repo terrain entry
Service ExecStart points to a venv python and an in-repo engine, never naming /home/tim/CosyVoice directly
concerns: Status is unconfirmed despite documented service activity and recent reads
Coverage only two files for a 5.3G repo, leaving most contents uninspected
Model weights live under pretrained_models inside the repo and may not be git-versioned
No explicit commit hash or model checksum is recorded
notes: This entry maps an external TTS repo and its local model snapshot to the Company voice service through sys.path injection.
questions: Is the model directory under git or downloaded separately?
What exact commit hash is currently checked out?
How is the cosyvoice venv created and what package versions are pinned?
Does the engine modify any files inside /home/tim/CosyVoice at runtime?
Why is status unconfirmed when read dates and service references are present?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to describe the external CosyVoice repo and model weights used by the voice engine; the file does exactly that, including the indirect launch path.
apparent_themes: Runtime path injection for upstream libraries
intention_signals: The entry is trying to make an opaque, large external TTS dependency discoverable by recording its location, size, model snapshot, and the indirect sys.path launch contract so the system can be reasoned about without inspecting the live filesystem.
novelty: It treats a git clone containing multi-gigabyte pretrained model weights as a terrain entity that is not directly executed but consumed via sys.path by an in-repo engine launched from a dedicated venv.
contribution: It preserves the exact location, size, upstream remote, model snapshot, port, service unit, and runtime import contract of the CosyVoice dependency, which a fused system would need to reproduce or audit the voice TTS setup.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain entry for the external CosyVoice repository and CosyVoice2-0.5B model weights at /home/tim/CosyVoice, consumed via sys.path by the Company voice engine launched on port 4126 by company-voice-cosyvoice.service using the cosyvoice venv.
intention_for_embedding: To document how the Company voice TTS service depends on an upstream CosyVoice checkout and its local model snapshot.
